source_docs.title: FIPRESCI Table S3: tagmentation reaction buffer compositions

oligos.name: RT PolyT Primer (NEB S1327S)
oligos.role: Reverse transcription primer (anchored oligo-dT) priming first-strand cDNA on polyadenylated mRNA
oligos.notes: Listed as d(T)23VN; V = A/C/G (not T), N = any base. Anchored oligo-dT.

oligos.name: RT random Primer (Thermo Scientific SO142)
oligos.role: Reverse transcription random hexamer primer (used alone or as equimolar mix with oligo-dT)

oligos.name: TN5_A_ME
oligos.role: Non-barcoded Tn5 mosaic-end (ME) adapter strand loaded into the i7-only transposome
oligos.notes: Synthesized as [5Phos]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT/3ddC/ : 5' phosphorylation; 3' dideoxy-C blocks polymerase extension. Reverse complement of the ME (AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG).

oligos.name: TN5_R2_index (TN5_B_index, round1-barcoded)
oligos.role: Barcoded Tn5 transposome strand: partial TruSeq Read2 + well-specific round1 barcode + mosaic end
oligos.notes: Structure = partial TruSeq Read2 (GACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT) + 6 bp round1 barcode (NNNNNN) + ME (AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG). 96 barcodes provided (n-Tn5-bo_i7-index1..96); e.g. index1 barcode AAAGAA -> GACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCTAAAGAAAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG; index96 barcode TTTGGG. Round1 barcode records well/sample identity.

oligos.name: Indexed i7-only Tn5 transposome adapter (annealed)
oligos.role: Double-stranded Tn5 adapter cassette (TN5_A_ME annealed to TN5_R2_index) coupled to TruePrep Tn5 to form the round1 indexed transposome

components.name: TN5_R2_index (top)
components.role: Barcoded strand: partial Read2 + round1 barcode + ME

components.name: TN5_A_ME (bottom)
components.role: Phosphorylated, 3'-ddC-blocked ME strand annealing to the ME of the top strand

oligos.notes: Annealed 1:1 at 10 uM then coupled to TruePrep Tagment Enzyme (Vazyme S601-01). i7-only transposome (homodimer of the same barcoded adapter).

oligos.name: 10x Genomics 5' Gel-bead barcoded TSO oligo
oligos.role: Gel-bead capture/template-switch oligo adding round2 cell barcode, UMI and TSO by template switching + cDNA extension
oligos.notes: From 10x Genomics Single Cell 5' RNA Reagent Kit. Structure = partial TruSeq Read1 (CTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT) + 16 bp round2 (10x) cell barcode + 10 bp UMI + TSO TTTCTTATAT-rGrGrG (riboG folded to GGG). Anneals to the 3' CCC of the C-tailed cDNA.

oligos.name: S5R-P5-bio
oligos.role: Biotinylated P5 primer for biotinylated single-primer linear pre-amplification of 5'-end cDNA
oligos.notes: [5Bio] 5' biotin; enables streptavidin capture. Anneals to the Read1/partial-Read1 region introduced by the gel-bead oligo.

oligos.name: S5R-P5
oligos.role: P5 forward primer for final library amplification (P5 + partial TruSeq Read1)
oligos.notes: Non-biotinylated version used in the library-amplification PCR.

oligos.name: S-P7 (indexed P7 library primer, generic)
oligos.role: P7 reverse primer introducing i7 sample index; anneals to the partial Read2 / round1-adapter end
oligos.notes: Written as CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGAT[NNNNNN]GTGACTGGAGTTCAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATC-s-T (terminal phosphorothioate before the 3' T). [NNNNNN] = 6 bp i7 sample index (optional additional multiplexing).

oligos.name: S-P7-index1
oligos.role: P7 library primer with fixed i7 sample index CGTGAT

oligos.name: S-P7-index2
oligos.role: P7 library primer with fixed i7 sample index ACATCG

oligos.name: S-P7-index3
oligos.role: P7 library primer with fixed i7 sample index GCCTAA

oligos.name: S-P7-index4
oligos.role: P7 library primer with fixed i7 sample index TGGTCA

oligos.name: S-P7-index5
oligos.role: P7 library primer with fixed i7 sample index CACTGT

oligos.name: S-P7-index6
oligos.role: P7 library primer with fixed i7 sample index ATTGGC

oligos.name: S-P7-index7
oligos.role: P7 library primer with fixed i7 sample index GATCTG

oligos.name: S-P7-index8
oligos.role: P7 library primer with fixed i7 sample index TCAAGT

oligos.name: S-P7-index9
oligos.role: P7 library primer with fixed i7 sample index CTGATC

oligos.name: S-P7-index10
oligos.role: P7 library primer with fixed i7 sample index AAGCTA

oligos.name: S-P7-index11
oligos.role: P7 library primer with fixed i7 sample index GTAGCC

oligos.name: S-P7-index12
oligos.role: P7 library primer with fixed i7 sample index TACAAG

oligos.name: Human TCR outer-1
oligos.role: Outer (first) nested-PCR primer for single-cell TCR VDJ enrichment

oligos.name: Human TCR outer-2
oligos.role: Outer (first) nested-PCR primer for single-cell TCR VDJ enrichment

oligos.name: Human TCR inner-1
oligos.role: Inner (second) nested-PCR primer for single-cell TCR VDJ enrichment

oligos.name: Human TCR inner-2
oligos.role: Inner (second) nested-PCR primer for single-cell TCR VDJ enrichment

final_library.annotated_library_sequence: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGA(P5) GATCTACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT(TruSeq Read1) [CELL_BARCODE:16](round2 10x CB) [UMI:10] TTTCTTATATGGG(TSO) [CDNA](5'-end cDNA insert) CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT(ME) [CELL_BARCODE:6](round1 Tn5 barcode) AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTC(partial TruSeq Read2) TGAACTCCAGTCAC [SAMPLE_INDEX:6](i7) ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG(P7)
annotation_lines: GATCTACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT — TruSeq Read1 sequencing primer region (introduced via gel-bead oligo + S5R-P5)
[CELL_BARCODE:6] — round1 well/sample barcode added by indexed Tn5

library_generation.title: In-situ reverse transcription
library_generation.summary: Poly-dT (or random) primer and MMLV reverse transcriptase make first-strand cDNA on mRNA inside permeabilized cells or nuclei.
library_generation.note: Fixed/permeabilized cells or nuclei are reverse transcribed in situ with Maxima H Minus (M-MLV) using RT PolyT Primer (or random hexamer, or a mix). The RNA/cDNA hybrid is retained inside the cell.

library_generation.title: Terminal-transferase C-tailing
library_generation.summary: M-MLV adds 2-5 untemplated C nucleotides to the 3' end of the newly synthesized cDNA.
library_generation.note: The terminal transferase activity of MMLV appends extra C's (CCC) that later serve as the template-switch landing site for the gel-bead TSO.

library_generation.title: Indexed i7-only Tn5 tagmentation (round1 barcode)
library_generation.summary: A well-specific i7-only Tn5 transposome cuts the RNA/DNA hybrid, inserting the round1 barcode + mosaic end.
library_generation.note: Performed in a 96/384-well plate (one indexed transposome per well). The Tn5 stays bound to its RNA/cDNA substrate (no SDS) so contiguity is preserved until droplet capture. Adds partial TruSeq Read2 + 6 bp round1 barcode to the 5' fragment end.

library_generation.title: Droplet template switching (round2 barcode + UMI)
library_generation.summary: Inside 10x GEMs the gel-bead TSO templates cDNA extension, adding the round2 cell barcode, UMI and TSO.
library_generation.note: Pre-indexed cells/nuclei are overloaded into 10x Chromium droplets. The gel-bead barcoded TSO base-pairs with the 3' CCC of the cDNA; extension incorporates the 16 bp round2 (droplet) barcode + 10 bp UMI + Read1 handle. Round1(well)+round2(droplet) together uniquely tag each cell.

library_generation.title: Break emulsion and purify
library_generation.summary: Droplets are broken and the barcoded double-stranded cDNA products are recovered and cleaned up.
library_generation.note: Recovery Agent + Dynabeads MyOne Silane cleanup after GEM-RT incubation.

library_generation.title: Biotinylated single-primer linear pre-amplification
library_generation.summary: S5R-P5-bio linearly amplifies the 5'-end cDNA and adds the P5 arm; products are streptavidin-captured.
library_generation.note: Biotinylated single-primer (linear) amplification enriches 5'-end cDNA; biotin enables Dynabeads MyOne Streptavidin C1 capture before library amplification.

library_generation.title: Library amplification (P5 + i7 S-P7)
library_generation.summary: S5R-P5 and an indexed S-P7 primer amplify the captured cDNA, appending P7 and the optional i7 sample index.
library_generation.note: Final PCR with S5R-P5 (forward) and S-P7-index (reverse) completes the P5...P7 flow-cell library and can add a 6 bp i7 sample index for additional multiplexing.

library_sequencing.note: Read1 primer is identical to the top-strand partial Read1 and anneals to the bottom strand; its 3′ end extends rightward into the gel-bead barcode block, reading the 16 bp round2 (10x droplet) cell barcode then the 10 bp UMI (26 cycles). 150-bp PE on NovaSeq 6000 / MGISeq-T7.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read1 primer 5'-...CTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT----------------------------->
             5'-...CTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT[== round2 CB 16 nt ==][= UMI 10 nt =]TTTCTTATATGGG[cDNA]...-3'  (top)
             3'-...GAGAAAGGGATGTGCTGCGAGAAGGCTAGA[== round2 CB 16 nt ==][= UMI 10 nt =]AAAGAATATACCC[cDNA]...-5'  (bottom)
                                                 |___________ R1 reads CB(16)+UMI(10) = 26 nt ___________|

library_sequencing.note: Read2 primer is identical to the bottom strand and anneals to the top-strand partial-Read2 region (AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTC); its 3′ end extends leftward, reading the 6 bp round1 (Tn5 well) barcode, then the 19 bp mosaic end, then into the 5′-end cDNA insert. FASTQs are demultiplexed by the round1 barcode and the BC+ME are trimmed before Cell Ranger.
library_sequencing.diagram:
5'-...[cDNA]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT[round1 BC 6 nt]AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTC...-3'  (top)
3'-...[cDNA]GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGA[round1 BC 6 nt]TCTAGCCTTCTCGTGTGCAG...-5'  (bottom)
   <-------------------------------------------TCTAGCCTTCTCGTGTGCAG-5'  Read2 primer
      |__ R2 reads BC1(6) + ME(19) + cDNA (150 cycles) __|

library_sequencing.primer: i7 index sequencing primer (5′-...GACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT-3′ read2/P7 region)
library_sequencing.note: Optional i7 sample-index read, present only when an indexed S-P7 primer is used for an extra round of sample multiplexing. Primer abuts the sample index and reads 6 cycles toward P7.
library_sequencing.diagram:
5'-...CACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC[i7 index 6 nt]ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG-3'  (top)
                        3'-...GTGTGCAGACTTGAGGTCAGTG[i7 index 6 nt]TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC-5'  (bottom)
i7 index primer 5'-...GTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT...CTCCAGTCAC-------->
                                                    |__ I1 reads i7 index (6 cycles) __|

title: FIPRESCI
description: FIPRESCI (FIve PRime End Single-cell Combinatorial Indexing RNA sequencing) is a droplet-based combinatorial-indexing method for massive-scale 5′-end scRNA-seq and snRNA-seq. Whole transcriptomes are pre-indexed in situ inside permeabilized cells or nuclei: reverse transcription with an oligo-dT (or random) primer creates RNA/cDNA hybrids that an i7-only indexed Tn5 transposome tagments in a 96/384-well plate, adding a well-specific round1 barcode plus a mosaic-end/partial-Read2 adapter. Pooled, overloaded cells are then encapsulated on a 10x Genomics 5′ Chromium platform where a gel-bead TSO adds a droplet-specific round2 (16 bp) barcode and 10 bp UMI by template switching; the combination of round1+round2 uniquely resolves single cells even with multiple cells per droplet. Reads capture the 5′-end/TSS of transcripts (enabling promoter/enhancer and TCR/BCR repertoire analysis), with R1 reading the round2 cell barcode + UMI and R2 reading the round1 barcode + cDNA.

original_publication.title: FIPRESCI: droplet microfluidics based combinatorial indexing for massive-scale 5′-end single-cell RNA sequencing

throughput.summary: Increases droplet-microfluidic throughput >10-fold over standard 10x 5' scRNA-seq; ~100,000 single-cell transcriptomes per single Chromium channel with low collision. A single experiment can be completed by one person in 12 h.
throughput.cells: 58,771 cells and 59,622 nuclei recovered from 100k loaded (3 cell lines, >58% recovery); 117,804 nuclei from one 10x channel for E10.5 mouse embryo; 8,049 species-mixing cells at 0.2% collision rate (vs expected 7.6% multiplet); 53,183 T cells (41,377 after integration) across 14 donors.
throughput.rna: 5'-end transcript / TSS capture; median 2,239 (mean 2,150) genes per cell with oligo-dT at 50k fragments/cell; ~2% of reads from eRNA loci; identifies 3,898-4,685 eRNA loci.
throughput.dna: Not applicable — RNA-only assay (5'-end transcriptome); no separate DNA/ATAC library is generated, though chromatin accessibility is computationally predicted from eRNA signal via BABEL.

publication.statistical_model: Droplet loading modeled as a Poisson-like distribution (combinatorial indexing resolves within-droplet doublets); SCTransform regularized negative-binomial regression used for expression normalization; Wilcoxon tests for differential expression and Fisher's exact test for differential TSS usage.

other.label: Key enzymology
other.value: Tn5 transposome tagments RNA/DNA hybrids and stays bound to substrate (contiguity preserved) until dissociated by SDS

other.label: Platform / kits
other.value: 10x Genomics Chromium Single Cell 5' (V(D)J v1.1 / 5' v2); TruePrep Tn5 (Vazyme S601-01); sequenced on Illumina NovaSeq 6000 or MGISeq-T7, 150-bp PE

other.label: Multimodal capability
other.value: Compatible with paired single-cell TCR/BCR VDJ profiling via nested PCR; applied to pan-cancer peripheral T cells from 14 donors

other.label: Data availability
other.value: Genome Sequence Archive PRJCA007160 (NGDC/CNCB); code at github.com/hz1010/FIPRESCI_data_analysis

other.label: Recommended tagmentation buffer
other.value: Customized Tris-based DMF buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 5 mM MgCl2, 10% DMF); crowding agents (PEG) reduced in-cell performance

data_processing.summary: FASTQs are demultiplexed by the round1 (Tn5 well) barcode with zero mismatch; each round1 group is then run through Cell Ranger as a standard 10x 5′ library, and round1+round2 barcodes are concatenated in Seurat to form single-cell barcodes and a merged count matrix for standard normalization, clustering, annotation, and downstream TSS/eRNA and TCR analyses.
data_processing.statistical_model: SCTransform regularized negative-binomial regression for normalization; Wilcoxon rank-sum test for differential expression; Fisher's exact test for differential TSS usage; combinatorial round1+round2 indexing resolves Poisson-distributed multi-cell droplet loading.
